value.body:
## Context

When an agent works inside the Cycle ticket system, it needs typed MCP actions for status, assignment, comments, records, dependencies, artifacts, and handoff. The current tool surface is useful but not yet a complete execution API.

## Outcome

Cycle MCP becomes the stable agent-facing control plane for executing ticket work without raw file edits to ticket metadata.

## Acceptance criteria

- MCP exposes operations to assign/claim tickets, update status, update priority, add comments, add linked records, manage dependencies, list templates, and attach run artifacts.
- MCP operations return resolvable `cycle://` references where applicable.
- All write operations take idempotency/request ids and return structured errors.
- Tool schemas are narrow and schema-derived from Cycle contracts.
- Agents can perform a complete ticket lifecycle through MCP without direct GitDB/ticket-file manipulation.
- Tests cover tool schema validation, authorization/approval behavior, and failure envelopes.

frontmatter.title: Expose full agent ticket lifecycle operations through Cycle MCP

value.title: Expose full agent ticket lifecycle operations through Cycle MCP
